result.markdown:
--- title: "Functional connectivity" subtitle: "Seed-to-target correlation on the typed fmrimod substrate." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- Functional connectivity is not a core fmrimod seam — there is no `fm.connectivity()`. What fmrimod *does* give you is the typed substrate the analysis should sit on: a dataset that owns its timing, a **typed nuisance model** instead of a hand-built matrix, projection through the same `residualize` used by the first-level path, and the effect-size transforms used for group inference. The only step without an fmrimod primitive is the Pearson correlation itself, and that stays one explicit line. ## A Dataset, Not Bare Arrays Start from an `fmri_dataset`. It carries the data, the TR, and the sampling frame together, so every downstream step reads its timing from one typed source instead of re-deriving it. ::: {#202f09c3 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod import baseline_model, design_matrix from fmrimod.residualize import residualize rng = np.random.default_rng(13) n_time, n_vox = 160, 9 # Voxel 0 is the seed; voxels 1-3 are genuinely coupled to it. data = rng.normal(size=(n_time, n_vox)) data[:, 1:4] += 0.45 * data[:, 0:1] motion = rng.normal(size=(n_time, 6)) # e.g. realignment parameters dataset = fm.fmri_dataset(data, tr=2.0) dataset.n_timepoints, dataset.n_voxels ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` (160, 9) ``` ::: ::: ## A Typed Nuisance Model The drift, intercept, and motion confounds are a **typed `baseline_model`**, not `np.column_stack([...])`. The model owns the column structure; you never hand-assemble the nuisance matrix. ::: {#3df9148a .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} bmodel = baseline_model( basis="poly", degree=2, sframe=dataset.sampling_frame, intercept="runwise", nuisance_list=[motion], ) design_matrix(bmodel).shape # (time, drift + intercept + motion columns) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` (160, 9) ``` ::: ::: ## Residualize Through the Typed Path `residualize` dispatches on the typed model — pass `bmodel` itself, not its matrix. This is the same projection the first-level seam uses, so the cleaned signal is consistent with how fmrimod fits everything else. ::: {#6fae54c5 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} clean = residualize(bmodel, dataset.get_all_data()) clean.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ``` (160, 9) ``` ::: ::: ## Seed-to-Target Correlation This is the one step with no fmrimod primitive — fmrimod models signals, it does not own a connectivity estimator. Keep it to one explicit, readable line on the *cleaned* signal. ::: {#c9be1362 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} seed = clean[:, 0] targets = clean[:, 1:] sc = seed - seed.mean() tc = targets - targets.mean(axis=0) r = (sc @ tc) / (np.sqrt((sc**2).sum()) * np.sqrt((tc**2).sum(axis=0))) pd.DataFrame({ "target": [f"roi_{j + 1}" for j in range(targets.shape[1])], "r": r, }).round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
target r
0 roi_1 0.387
1 roi_2 0.403
2 roi_3 0.379
3 roi_4 0.258
4 roi_5 0.092
5 roi_6 -0.124
6 roi_7 0.095
7 roi_8 -0.008
``` ::: ::: ## Back to fmrimod for Group-Ready Effect Sizes `r_to_z` is the fmrimod effect-size API: it returns the Fisher *z* and its sampling variance `1 / (n - 3)`, which is exactly what the group seam (`fmri_meta`, `group_fit`) expects per connection. ::: {#8ee3abca .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} z, z_var = fm.r_to_z(r, dataset.n_timepoints) pooled_r = fm.z_to_r(z.mean()) pd.DataFrame({ "fisher_z": z, "z_variance": z_var, }).round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
fisher_z z_variance
0 0.408 0.006
1 0.427 0.006
2 0.399 0.006
3 0.264 0.006
4 0.093 0.006
5 -0.125 0.006
6 0.095 0.006
7 -0.008 0.006
``` ::: ::: ::: {#127ab802 .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} round(float(z.mean()), 3), round(float(pooled_r), 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` (0.194, 0.192) ``` ::: ::: The coupled targets (`roi_1`–`roi_3`) carry the visible correlation; the Fisher *z* and its variance are now in the shape the group-level tools consume. Motion correction, registration, and normalization remain outside fmrimod's scope — the substrate here begins at a preprocessed dataset. ## Next - [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd) - [Datasets](datasets.qmd)
